response.justification: Wei Chen is a capable polyglot engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background and broad systems experience, but the role demands deep Java/Kotlin expertise, JVM internals knowledge, and hands-on experience with the Spring ecosystem — none of which are evident in this resume. Java appears in the skills list and in passing at Altom (robotic arms) and National Instruments, but these are peripheral uses rather than a core competency. There is no mention of Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise JVM framework, and the single relevant IDE role (NI LabWindows/CVI) was brief and C-based. The candidate's genuine strengths lie in a direction orthogonal to this position's requirements.
strengths: Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and IDE internals through NI LabWindows/CVI work and open-source test tooling projects (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Polyglot engineer with proven ability to operate across stacks, including low-level systems work (DLang async I/O, C/C++ audio synthesis, embedded systems)
Strong track record of software craftsmanship practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — relevant to any engineering role
concerns: No Java or Kotlin proficiency at a level consistent with this role — primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js; Java usage is incidental and dated
Zero evidence of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any enterprise JVM framework experience
No JVM internals knowledge demonstrated; IDE experience is limited to a brief C-based tooling role at NI, not JVM language tooling

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise JVM frameworks
